title: API token exposed via /api/get_config
description: The endpoint /api/get_config returns the default API token, allowing any client to retrieve privileged credentials.
root_cause_analysis: The GET endpoint includes DEFAULT_API_TOKEN in the JSON response without any access control.
suggested_fix: Remove default_token from the response or protect the endpoint with authentication.
resolution: Removed default_token from /api/get_config response; frontend now defaults to empty token.

title: Shell commands executed with shell=True allow injection
description: Cells beginning with `!` run commands via subprocess.Popen with shell=True, which can be abused if notebook content is untrusted.
root_cause_analysis: Kernel worker runs subprocess.Popen(cmd, shell=True, ...) without sanitization.
suggested_fix: Execute commands with shell=False using shlex.split or whitelist allowed commands.
resolution: Replaced shell=True with shlex.split(cmd) + shell=False in kernel_worker.

title: Global mutable kernel state may cause race conditions
description: Globals cmd_queue, res_queue, kernel_process and cached_variables are mutable and not thread-safe, risking state corruption when handling concurrent requests.
root_cause_analysis: Kernel is managed via module-level globals without synchronization.
suggested_fix: Encapsulate kernel management in a class with proper locking or use a process pool.
resolution: Kernel state remains encapsulated in KernelManager, and run_cell now executes via a single serialized execute() path guarded by an execution lock so concurrent requests cannot interleave send/get_result or corrupt cached variable state.

title: No authentication for notebook and API endpoints
description: All routes are publicly accessible, allowing anyone with network access to read/write notebooks and invoke AI calls.
root_cause_analysis: Flask app has no auth middleware.
suggested_fix: Add token‑based or session‑based authentication and protect write endpoints.
resolution: Intentionally not adding authentication; project is designed as a local/dev tool without auth requirements.

title: Missing CORS configuration
description: No explicit CORS headers are set, which may cause cross‑origin request failures or unintended exposure.
root_cause_analysis: Flask default does not set Access-Control-Allow-Origin.
suggested_fix: Integrate Flask‑CORS and configure allowed origins.
resolution: Added CORS(app) to Flask app and installed flask-cors.

title: is_safe_path does not resolve symlinks, potential traversal
description: Path validation uses os.path.abspath but not os.path.realpath, allowing symlink attacks to escape the workspace directory.
root_cause_analysis: Function only checks that the absolute path starts with WORKSPACE_DIR.
suggested_fix: Use os.path.realpath (or pathlib.resolve) before prefix check to block symlink traversal.
resolution: Replaced os.path.abspath with os.path.realpath in both WORKSPACE_DIR and is_safe_path.

title: Monolithic app.py hinders maintainability
description: All routes, kernel logic, and utilities reside in a single file, making future extensions difficult.
root_cause_analysis: Project started as a prototype and was never refactored into modules.

code_snippets: # Large file with 723 lines combining Flask routes, kernel worker, GPU telemetry, and file APIs

suggested_fix: Split into distinct modules (e.g., routes/, services/, utils/) and import them in a lightweight entry point.
resolution: Split the monolithic 991-line app.py into a core/ package: errors.py (AppError hierarchy), kernel.py (KernelManager + kernel_worker), gpu.py (pynvml telemetry), utils.py (is_safe_path), and routes/ containing six Flask Blueprints (static, gpu, kernel, ai, lint, file). app.py is now a slim entry point that loads config, instantiates mutable runtime state (kernel_manager/WORKSPACE_DIR/is_safe_path), and wires up error handlers + Blueprints. The entry module is exposed to Blueprints via app.config['_STATE_MODULE'] so views read monkeypatchable module-level state at request time without `import app` cycles, keeping the existing pytest suite green (5/5 passing).

title: Missing automated tests
description: Repository lacks unit or integration tests for kernel execution, API endpoints, and utility functions.
root_cause_analysis: No test directory or CI configuration present.
suggested_fix: Add a pytest suite covering kernel worker, API routes, and file operations; integrate with CI.
resolution: Added pytest configuration and a test suite covering config exposure, lint syntax errors, run_cell delegation with a fake kernel manager, file save/read round trip, and symlink path traversal protection. README now documents pytest installation and execution.

title: Broad exception handling masks specific errors
description: Many routes catch generic Exception and return generic 500 responses, losing useful error context.
suggested_fix: Define custom exception types and map them to appropriate HTTP status codes with clear messages.
resolution: Introduced a custom exception hierarchy (AppError base class, KernelError, FileStorageError, UpstreamAPIError) with structured error_code and status_code fields. Registered a Flask @app.errorhandler(AppError) to convert any AppError subclass into a consistent JSON response. Replaced all bare 'except Exception' blocks with specific exception handlers (PermissionError, OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, RuntimeError, requests.exceptions.ConnectionError/Timeout/RequestException), each raising the appropriate custom exception type with a clear message and correct HTTP status code (403, 422, 502, 503, 504).

title: Kernel process recreation adds latency
description: When the kernel process dies, the next request spawns a new process, causing a cold start delay.
root_cause_analysis: ensure_kernel creates a new process each time it detects a dead worker.
suggested_fix: Implement a watchdog to keep the kernel warm or maintain a pool of ready workers.
resolution: Added KernelWatchdog: a background daemon thread (_watchdog_loop) polls kernel health every 2 seconds and proactively calls _spawn_kernel() if the process has died, so the kernel is always warm before the next request. Kernel is also pre-started at app boot via warm_start(). A new /api/kernel_status endpoint exposes kernel and watchdog liveness. Watchdog is stopped cleanly via atexit.
